news.title:
Osho Krishan of Angel One recommends buying Hindustan Zinc and Chennai Petroleum shares based on technical indicators and favorable setups.
Nifty 50 and Sensex declined on April 2 due to global tensions; FPIs sold ₹83.3B, while DIIs bought ₹71.7B.

news.title:

Dividend reflects strong financials amidst oil sector challenges, boosting investor confidence.

news.title:

The move indicates a focus on shareholder returns and investor interest.

news.title:
Chennai Petroleum to consider interim dividend on 26 Mar'26. Q3 FY26 profit surged to Rs 987.2 cr from Rs 10.5 cr YoY.
Revenue rose 21.3% YoY to Rs 15,683.2 cr in Q3 FY26. Stock fell 5.7% to Rs 1,004 on BSE.
news.summary:
Chennai Petroleum to consider interim dividend on 26 Mar'26. Q3 FY26 profit surged to Rs 987.2 cr from Rs 10.5 cr YoY.
Revenue rose 21.3% YoY to Rs 15,683.2 cr in Q3 FY26. Stock fell 5.7% to Rs 1,004 on BSE.

news.title:
Chennai Petroleum Corp to hold a board meeting on 26th Mar 2026 to consider interim dividend for FY 2025-26.
Trading window for CPCL securities closed from 21st Mar 2026 till 48 hours post board meeting.
news.summary:
Chennai Petroleum Corp to hold a board meeting on 26th Mar 2026 to consider interim dividend for FY 2025-26.

Key facilities rated include cash credit, fund-based facilities, and commercial papers.

news.title:
OMCs consider freezing refinery transfer price to limit losses, impacting standalone refiners like MRPL, CPCL, and HMEL.
Private refiners like Reliance and Nayara may face margin squeeze; retail fuel prices have remained unchanged since April 2022.
news.summary:

news.title:
OMC shares rise as Brent falls 4% to $66 and WTI below $63 on easing US-Iran tensions.

news.title:
Chennai Petroleum surged 4.9% post Q3 results, with net profit up 4719.5% YoY to Rs. 1001.5 Cr and revenue rising 21% YoY to Rs. 15,683 Cr.
CPCL's low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.22, 3-year average ROE of 31.1, and P/E of 5.99 indicate strong fundamentals and potential undervaluation.
news.summary:
Chennai Petroleum surged 4.9% post Q3 results, with net profit up 4719.5% YoY to Rs. 1001.5 Cr and revenue rising 21% YoY to Rs. 15,683 Cr.
CPCL's low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.22, 3-year average ROE of 31.1, and P/E of 5.99 indicate strong fundamentals and potential undervaluation.

news.title:
428 companies report Dec-2025 results; mid, small caps show strong earnings momentum.
Chennai Petroleum Corp Ltd stands out with exceptional growth in key financial metrics.
news.summary:
428 companies report Dec-2025 results; mid, small caps show strong earnings momentum.

news.title:
Chennai Petroleum's Q3 FY26 crude throughput rose to 2.79 MMT, with 105% capacity utilization.
Revenue surged to ₹19,438 crore vs ₹15,683 crore, and PAT hit ₹987 crore vs ₹10 crore YoY.
news.summary:
Chennai Petroleum's Q3 FY26 crude throughput rose to 2.79 MMT, with 105% capacity utilization.
Revenue surged to ₹19,438 crore vs ₹15,683 crore, and PAT hit ₹987 crore vs ₹10 crore YoY.
